description_long: This house features a two-story structure with a muted green facade and brown trim, topped by a prominent, steeply pitched orange gable roof with a slight upward curve at the eaves. The front elevation displays two small dormer windows projecting from the roof, adding architectural interest and natural light to the upper floor. The windows are symmetrically arranged with brown frames and sills, and the main entryway is marked by an arched door with a small overhang supported by posts. The side elevations reveal the depth of the building and the overhanging roof, while the rear is simpler, lacking windows or doors. The house sits on a slightly raised foundation, and the overall design suggests a blend of traditional and East Asian architectural influences. The materials appear to be primarily wood and plaster, with a tiled or shingled roof.
description: A two-story house with a steep orange gable roof, green walls, brown trim, and symmetrical dormer windows. The main entry features an arched door with a small overhang.
description_view_0: Side view showing the steep gable roof, two dormer windows, and symmetrical window placement on the lower floors.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main arched entry door, two dormer windows, and symmetrical facade details.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible roof overhang, dormer windows, and side wall details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain back wall, roof overhang, and absence of windows or doors.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched main door with a small overhang supported by posts, no steps or porch.
